mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate suppresses the risk-free rate and equity discount rates, mechanically raising present values of future corporate cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in real retail sales signals weakening consumer demand, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS declines > 1.5% MoM over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and falling industrial production indicate a joint deterioration in labor markets and output, compounding recession risk and driving negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp over 2 months while INDPRO declines over the same period
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling INDPRO does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by low unemployment, a decline in housing starts signals that higher borrowing costs and exhausted demand are curbing construction activity, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: HOUST declines during UNRATE below 5% do not coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and revenue capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not occur in more than 55% of months when INDPRO increases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings expectations and pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.4% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) following months where CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in payroll employment and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports top-line revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and RSAFS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint positive signal of PAYEMS and RSAFS does not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of co-movement instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected drop in nonfarm payrolls signals immediate weakness in household income and consumption prospects, triggering rapid risk-off repositioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when PAYEMS declines month-over-month over any 2-year rolling window.
